blocks.content: They slowed at Birch without deciding to, the way they always did, because there was a hill on the other side that was better coasted than climbed.
She asked it looking at the road. Calm. The way Daisy asked things that weren't calm.
Kit thought about the answer — actually thought about it, which was rare for him.
Daisy nodded. Kept her eyes on the road.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I don't know. You tell me.
She didn't answer for a long second. They crested the hill and the street opened up, the long flat stretch toward her block.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I just don't want things to be different.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: They don't have to be different.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Things are already a little different.
He wanted to argue with that and couldn't, because she was right, and they both knew it, and the things that were true between them had a way of being said even when he was trying not to say them.
